reviews.review_text:
My favorite Total War, the best in my opinion and has the most complete experience from beginning to end.

The main base campaign has everything you want from a strategy game. Interesting choices, robust economy and civil management, multiple viable strategies, great battles and the best diplomacy in the total war franchise period. Moreover the campaign's arc is always able to hit the right note from the beginning with everyone starting out to the climax as the game consolidates to the titular three kingdoms. It even bows out at the right time as well making every victory earned and every campaign a satisfyingly experience. 

It is also highly replayable as well with each faction bringing new mechanics, playstyles and incentives that makes drastically different experiences. Comparing Cao Cao and Dong Zhou's campaign is like night and day! Moreover a lot of core mechanics sell the time period and era so well, makes the player feel like they are in the 14th century epic remaking China to his will.

Innovative and complete, this game is a no brainer recommendation from me.

This game when it first came out was honestly somewhat of a disappointment. It had some great ideas but it just wasn’t quite there. However in 2026 it has truly become my favorite total war game. If you love the three kingdoms period this is an incredible game! I highly suggest people if you do get it play it with mods (check out the new general collection). While a lot don’t have English support honestly the sheer level of quality and enthusiasm the modders have is incredible they have completely changed the game! 

Just an example with the new general collection it adds tons of new characters/events/skills/models/units/difficulty it’s incredible. It reminds me a lot of the DEI mod for Rome 2 with its scale. I just started a new Lu Bu campaign and it’s become my favorite campaign so far. I’m constantly desperately fighting to survive, having to make real strategic decisions every turn and trying to use Lu Bu and his retinue under constant pressure to desperately hold on. It has been so much fun actually having the ai gang up on me and make smarter choices both on the campaign and in battles rather than just steam rolling the ai every campaign. Incredible game and incredible mod it’s awesome I highly recommend it! While the mods in the playlist  do not have English support it really is fine 70% of the game is still English and most of the Chinese is recognizable sentences after a few turns. For the rest just use a translator (I use the one on my phone) for the events and such. Can’t recommend the game and the mod playlist enough. If you love the three kingdoms period, or want really fun unique characters that actually form relationships with the rest of the characters based off of how you play, or just want a great rts/strategy game this is the game for you!

The game recommends playing Cao Cao because he's “easy mode”
→ immediately throws you into the middle of four big-dong warlords, ready to beat the sh*t out of you.

Now I understand why Cao Cao says: “Betray the world before it betrays you.”
The world just straight up hates you, bro..

You can basically send Lü Bu alone in a fully stacked enemy army and still win. Its unfortunate hes so strong that no enemy generals dare to duel him. Peak Total war! Especially enjoyed the city building in this part more than in the others

As a history and total war enjoyer, please play this game. Its bad, like really bad, like omg why does this game suck so hard to keep my public order and make copious amounts of money bad. As the great general Sun Tzu once said, "♥♥♥♥ this ♥♥♥♥ I'm out." You may feel like the French with the amount of retreating because the AI cheats and spawns armies out of thin air. Or you cannot trade with your allies because you have limited trade agreements and they are trading with Cao Cao and feeding his endless war against Wu and Shu while Lu Bu is fingering your booty hole. This game almost ruined my baby boys ability to walk because I would kick him every time I had a rebellion. I now owe child support, alimony and my dealer David. David I'm sorry I couldn't  pay you and I robbed you at gun point. Over all 8/9 butterflies and 34/41 Ice T's
Absolutely one of the best games I have every played. It was my first Total War game so there was a bit of a learning curve but despite its quirks I'd give it a 9.5/10.

After uniting China, I have to say that I should never ever trust Creative Assembly ever again and that any product bought is dollars thrown at a company that does not give a ♥♥♥♥. 

That being said, Wow. I really did enjoy my time with Three Kingdoms. Historical music, thematic fictional filler characters that pad out the world nicely. The DLCs are complete aberrations but the base game is incredible.  ]
Game is optimized like ♥♥♥♥, crashes and frame drops even on varying settings but whatever. 

Goes on sale for ten dollars IIRC. Worth as a fan of the time period and the battles are very fun with unit diversity. Big fan of the romance and authentic options that follow both realistic and more novel paths.

I have not the slightest idea what I'm doing and this is still one of my favorite games of all time. Great visuals, interesting mix of tbs and rts. Great game. P.S, watch the oversimplified video. It'll help.

This game kept me engaged for 249 (or 475) hours in a single campaign, so I feel pretty obligated at this point to give it my recommendation. I think that if it has caught your interest enough to sift through reviews, then you're probably safe to make the purchase, and expect a lot of fun hours ahead. If you're already a fan of previous Total War titles or Romance of the Three Kingdoms titles, I think you'll enjoy this game for sure. 
Note: When I posted my review, the played time in my library said 475 hours, but the review added a timestamp that says 249 hours played, so I'll just consider that the accurate one, because I have no idea. 

What's crazy is that after 249 hours, I've only played as one free base game faction from the standard year 190 start. There's still a whole lot more fun to be had, even just from the base game, without any of the expansions, as each faction plays in a unique way, and I think several will be very interesting. For example, there's Gongsun Zan's white horse fellows, tied for the longest bow range in the game which could make for a really fun horse archer campaign, and there's Mulu (Nanman expansion) with a war elephant mount, war elephant troops, and tigers. While I completed my playthrough as a bandit faction, the other two seem fun with their ambush style. Each faction has unique troops and buildings, and often something like a unique talent tree. Some rulers have unique gimmicks for flavor, like Lu Bu's kill list and Dong Zhuo's intimidation. The factions are all different enough from each other to make each playthrough feel unique and interesting. 

If I were to point out a drawback, it would probably be how long it takes to play through a campaign. While it deserves praise for providing so much content, 249 hours dedicated to a single playthrough is quite a commitment. I know that there are different winning conditions depending on what faction you choose though; or at least sometimes; for example, to win as a member of the Nanman faction, you just have to unite the tribes and survive for 50 turns after that. I imagine this would be a lot quicker than my playthrough as a Yan Baihu, where I wound up uniting almost the whole map, and ended on roughly turn 132. It's around turn 90 where I noticed the turns were taking a long time, like maybe 90 minutes/turn, but I think as a Nanman ruler, you should be done by turn 90. Maybe some other factions have quicker campaigns as well. I know that not all base game factions can make a claim to emperor, for example. This would change things. 

Two really great resources I want to point out are the modding community and the quality guide videos. There are a lot of interesting mods for this game; whether you want graphics tweaks, gameplay tweaks, or just a bug fix patch. In the past, I've always found the best mod collection elsewhere, but for this game, I found that the best collection of mods is right here on Steam, by choosing community hub from the store page, and then workshop. For this videos, I found that Serious Trivia does some really great videos for this game. I really appreciated his first look at each faction, where he goes over all of their unique units, buildings, and mechanics, and plays through the first few turns. You might find them useful in determining who to play as your first faction. 

As far as specs and settings go, I played on a couple of years old gaming laptop, and I found that for me personally, the original graphics settings were too safe. The game knew I was on a laptop, and I guess that's why. My graphics card is an Nvidia GeForce RTX 3060, and I turned the troop size up to 80, and most of my settings up to ultra or pretty high. I didn't have a single issue except one. I had one crash to desktop during a battle, but probably not performance related. I ran my  process monitor through my task manager for a while, and something was running at 40% when at top useage. I believe it was the CPU, but it could've been RAM. Either way, my laptop handled those settings very well. That said, I'd be very careful about the larger troop sizes on modest machines like mine. 

The best single tip I can offer is that trebuchets (catapults) are king. Initially your generals are the powerhouses in the smaller battles, but by mid-game, you'll find things go a lot smoother with 3-4 catapult units on your strategist. While their fiery ammo doesn't do quite as much damage, I always use it because the fire is a big hit to troop morale and it often sends the troops running from the battlefield. 

If you have the time to sink it to it, I highly recommend
best historical total war,the cavalry charges,the mods like trom,tup,mtu made the game alive ,if u grow up with dynasty warriors or rotk etc ,this is the ultimate game for you
crashing on starting a game or loading, when it tries to load the game map. Crashing 100%, I reinstalled the steam and game and still not working.

its really good, mods make it my favorite total war. if my hours played are any sign, id recommend character mods because otherwise its perfect. seeing all the characters we know from the DW series reduced to generics is crazy. I use TROM+MTU and it bring characters ive never even heard of, which feeds into the education aspect of a historical title. it also has romance which gives you the similar gameplay to TWW or achilles if thats your flavor.

I love almost all TW titles but this one is stunning, even if struggle to learn it at the start point. It was like "what a hack is this, it's so weird". 

Amazing  graphics, landscapes, battle and campaign map, minimalistic units cards and characters. Romance mode is awesome. Duels are great. I really enjoy this game and have a lot of fun.

Some improvements needed for user interface (like if an event happens, gotta show which city it affected. I can't remember all the Chinese names)
But overall one of the greatest Total War games. The spy system is revolutionary

I love total war games and I have hundreds and hundreds of hours across many titles. I heard this one was special and it really is. Diplomacy is such an integral part of the game and is done so well. You absolutely must play the diplomacy game to survive and get ahead and it is really engaging. The highlight for me is the end game in the campaign, when 3 factions rise to enough power to declare themselves emperor and then the real wars begin. Powerful empires with doom stacks cutting a path to your capital while you do the same, rallying allies to assist in the last epic fight with all the resources you've built through the game being used. Truly satisfying taking the enemy capitals and winning. Battles are fun and unit variety is enough to keep things interesting (there is more variety than shogun). Thoroughly recommend.

My first entry to the Total War title games. I personally really like Three Kingdoms lores so I am biased to say the least. I think this is a fine product to compete with other three kingdom titles as it offers a different, hands on combat experience to its competition. But for me, it is difficult to learn as I have no prior experience on total games. Overall, I think this is a successful game. Recommend if you are looking to try a different type of three kingdoms games or you are a total war series fan and looking to experience a different art style and historical lores. 

7.5/10

The most fun thing is when you can recruit all the heroes belong the Shu, Wu or Wei factions, and send them to campaign across the map. 

And of course you can recreate history during the fall of the Han Dynasty.
Plus point if you already know Dynasty Warriors or Romance of Three Kingdoms historical novel.

So far, worst total war i have ever seen, i dont know what is worst between the AI spamming diplo offer every turn or the game spamming me with lowering the difficulty while im just sitting in my town developing my stuff or the lack of any genuine clear way to see my movement range or to see correctly enemies armies on the map etc etc for someone that have more than 2k hours on total war warhammer i can clearly see everything lacking in this title and all QoL mechanics missing too, also i dont need to know every time a faction enter at war with another etc but this game make sure i have to know it every new turn making me press escape like a mad man.

8.5/10 — The Han Dynasty collapses, warlords rise, and all of China descends into chaos.
Yet somehow, even in the middle of civil war, Cao Cao still demands another trade agreement.😅
One of the most flexible diplomacy mechanics in any total war title.
One of the best recent titles from the total war franchise
